16th July 2026 Q1FY27 Results Snapshot:-  
 
🚩Decent / Good / Solid👏😃🔥
1) Piramal Finance (Solid)(AUM up 25% PAT up 67%)(FY27 reinforces our confidence in delivering the guidance on AUM growth, PAT growth and RoAUM that we shared at the beginning of the year)

2) Polycab India (Decent)(Consistent)(Wires & Cables business delivered 39% YoY growth, supported by healthy domestic demand, strong execution under Project Spring, and higher channel sales growth)(Wires outpaced cables during the quarter, supported by strong on-ground execution)

3) WeWork India Management (Decent)
4) Jio Financial Services (Solid across all segments)
5) 360 ONE WAM (ARR AUM up 19%)(Wealth Management AUM up 24.2%)
6) Menon Bearings (Solid)
7) BHEL (Solid)(Margin Expansion)(Used to report loss every Q1)(This Q1 PAT positive)
8) GNA Axles (Solid)
9) Alacrity Securities
10) Borosil Renewables (Solid Margin Expansion)
11) Chembond Chemicals

🚩Bad / Poor / Weak 😠😡🤬      
1) Wipro (Q2FY27 Guidance IT Services revenue is expected to be in the range of $2,574–2,627 Mn, implying -1.5% to +0.5% sequential growth in CC)
2) Sterling and Wilson Renewable Energy 
3) Hathway Cable & Datacom
4) Atishay
5) Mahindra EPC Irrigation
6) Integra Engineering India
7) Heritage Foods (Margin down)(Milk Sales Volume 11.62 LLPD Stable YoY)(VAP revenue jumped 39.7% YoY to ₹563.6 Crore excluding consumer ghee and butter, VAP's contribution to overall revenue hit a record 44% (up from 36% in Q1FY26))

8) 5paisa Capital (Peers doing much much better)
9) CEAT (Margin down)(Revenue up 22%)(Healthy YoY volume growth across segments) (Management expects raw material costs to remain elevated in Q2 and will continue balancing pricing actions with cost discipline to protect margins
10) PNB Gilts

🚩Mixed / Neutral / Average🙄🆗😑     
1) Newgen Software Technologies (YoY good QoQ Weak)
2) NELCO
3) South Indian Bank
4) D. B. Corp (Margin Expansion)
5) Onward Technologies (Despite industry demand variability, the company maintained its FY27 guidance of double-digit revenue growth and double-digit EBITDA growth)
6) Tech Mahindra (In CC Terms up 6.6% YoY & up 2.6% QoQ)(TCV $1,078 Mn up 33.3% YoY)
7) ITC Hotels (RevPAR up 8%)(Revenue up 15% EBITDA up 19%)
8) Alok Industries (Solid Margin Expansion)
9) Muthoot Capital Services (Improvement)

Stay tuned for more updates

Ajmera Realty & Infra | Q1FY27 Business Update

Real Estate Update
- Realisation-driven growth.
- Healthy collections maintained.
- Launch pipeline intact.

Business Performance
- Sales value ₹146 crore.
- Sales value up 35% YoY.
- Collections ₹173 crore.
- Carpet area sold 43,737 sq. ft..
- Higher realisations boosted sales.
- Healthy customer engagement.
- Existing portfolio traction remained strong.

Operational Highlights
- No new project launches.
- Geopolitical challenges impacted demand.
- Elevated input costs persisted.
- Supply chain disruptions continued.
- Homebuyer sentiment remained cautious.

Management Outlook
- Deep launch pipeline ahead.
- Calibrated project execution.
- Strong project fundamentals.
- Diversified project portfolio.
- Confident on long-term growth.

Key Takeaway
- Ajmera Realty delivered strong realization-led sales growth in Q1FY27 despite lower volumes and no new launches. Management remains confident that its robust launch pipeline, disciplined execution and diversified portfolio will support long-term growth as market conditions improve.

Heritage Foods | Q1FY27 Results

Value-Added Products Growth
- Record quarterly revenue.
- VAP mix reached new high.
- Margins impacted by milk costs.

- Revenue ₹1,338.1 crore.
- Revenue up 18% YoY.

Business Highlights
- VAP revenue ₹563.6 crore.
- VAP contribution 44%.
- Consumer pack fats ₹632.2 crore.
- Fats contribution 49%.
- Nutrivet revenue ₹72.8 crore.

Operational Highlights
- Milk procurement 18.10 LLPD.

- Paneer volume up 33%.

Management Outlook
- Expanding farmer network.

- Heritage Foods delivered record revenue in Q1FY27, driven by robust growth in value-added products and premium categories. While elevated milk procurement costs pressured margins, continued investments in innovation, premiumisation, procurement and distribution position the company for stronger long-term growth as input costs normalize.

CEAT: APPROVES ₹1,205 CR CAPEX FOR 2-WHEELER TYRE EXPANSION 🛞

• 🏭 Approved ₹1,205 crore capex to expand 2-wheeler tyre manufacturing capacity

• 📈 Capacity to increase by 53,000 tyres/day, taking total capacity from 80,000 to ~1.33 lakh tyres/day

• ⚙️ Expansion to be implemented in phases by FY2031

• 📊 Existing capacity utilization is around 95%, prompting capacity augmentation

• 💰 Capex to be funded through a mix of internal accruals and debt

• 🌱 Expansion may include greenfield and/or brownfield facilities, based on internal assessment

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens long-term growth, addresses high capacity utilization, and enhances CEAT's position in the growing two-wheeler tyre market.)

SURAJ INDUSTRIES: SIGNS FRANCHISE TIE-UP WITH VINTAGE DISTILLERS 🍾

• 🤝 Entered into a franchise tie-up agreement with Vintage Distillers Ltd. for bottling & packaging of Country Liquor/Rajasthan Made Liquor (RML) in aseptic packs

• 🏭 Manufacturing to be carried out at the company's Ajmer, Rajasthan facility on a job-work basis

• 💰 Company will earn ₹80 per case (plus GST) as bottling charges

• 📦 Minimum production commitment of 7,000 cases per month

• 💵 Vintage Distillers will provide an interest-free security deposit of ₹10 lakh, adjustable against monthly bottling charges

• 📅 Agreement valid from 1 August 2026 to 31 March 2027

🟢 Impact: Positive (Improves plant utilization, ensures minimum production volumes, and generates steady bottling revenue.)

ATISHAY: BAGS ₹49.35 LAKH ORDER FROM NAGAUR CENTRAL CO-OPERATIVE BANK 🏦💳

• 📑 Received a ₹49.35 lakh work order from The Nagaur Central Co-operative Bank, Rajasthan

• 💳 Order covers supply, installation, commissioning & maintenance of Micro ATM devices for Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS)

• 📅 Order execution is scheduled to be completed by 15 August 2026

• 🌾 Strengthens Atishay's presence in the rural and cooperative banking ecosystem and supports financial inclusion initiatives

🟢 Impact: Positive (New order strengthens order book and expands presence in the cooperative banking segment.)

AJMERA REALTY: Q1FY27 SALES VALUE RISES 35% YoY TO ₹146 CR 🏗️

• 💰 Sales value grew 35% YoY to ₹146 crore

• 💵 Collections stood at ₹173 crore (down 26% YoY)

• 📐 Carpet area sold declined 31% YoY to 43,737 sq. ft.

• 📈 Higher sales value despite lower volumes, driven by strong realizations across projects

• 🚀 Company remains confident of long-term growth, backed by a strong launch pipeline and robust portfolio across key micro-markets

CLEAN SCIENCE: SUBSIDIARY SIGNS STRATEGIC GLOBAL COLLABORATION WITH SWITZERLAND'S GENEUS CHEM 🌍🧪

• 🤝 Wholly owned subsidiary Clean-Fino Chem signed a long-term strategic manufacturing & supply agreement with Switzerland-based Geneus Chem AG

• 🧪 Partnership to manufacture advanced HALS (Hindered Amine Light Stabilizers) products on an exclusive worldwide basis

• 🌍 Products will be manufactured in India and supplied to both domestic and international markets

• 📈 As part of the collaboration, Geneus Chem will issue share warrants that can increase Clean-Fino Chem's stake to 25% in Geneus Chem upon exercise within 4 years (nominal value payable only on exercise)

• 🚀 Collaboration is expected to strengthen Clean Science's specialty chemicals portfolio and expand its global HALS business

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens global specialty chemicals presence, secures long-term manufacturing business, and provides strategic equity upside.)

Dixon has signed a binding term sheet for a 51:49 JV with Vivo India which is not a small player, but the #2 brand in the country with ~22% market share. On the Q4 FY26 call, management said they're very close to FDI approval. 

Once that clears, this one JV alone adds 20-22 million units a year to Dixon's smartphone volumes.

Put that number next to FY26 smartphone output of ~32 million units. This isn't a bolt-on it more than doubles the base. Dixon stops being just India's largest EMS player and starts owning the manufacturing backbone of the country's second-biggest phone brand.

IRAN THREATENS SECOND GLOBAL ENERGY CHOKEPOINT

Iran has reportedly told Yemen’s Houthis to be ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait if the U.S. strikes Iranian power infrastructure, according to Reuters sources.

With the Strait of Hormuz already shut, a disruption in the Red Sea would hit the Middle East’s two main oil export routes simultaneously, raising the risk of a severe global energy shock.

Sources say the Houthis have positioned missiles and drones near the Red Sea gateway and are awaiting orders. Saudi Arabia is reportedly treating the threat seriously as tensions between Tehran and Washington continue to escalate.

GE POWER INDIA: APPROVES DEMERGER OF DURGAPUR UNIT TO JSW ENERGY 🔄⚡

• 🏭 Demerger of the Durgapur business unit to JSW Energy to focus on the high-margin services business and exit an underutilized facility

• 🎁 GE Power India shareholders to receive 10 JSW Energy shares for every 139 GE Power India shares, while retaining their existing GE Power India holdings

• 📉 Durgapur facility incurred ~₹27 crore average annual losses, with better utilization expected under JSW Energy

• 📈 Core services order book grew at ~25% CAGR, from ₹299 crore (FY22) to ₹734 crore (FY26)

• 💹 Financial turnaround: Net worth ₹483 crore, EBITDA ₹277 crore, liquidity improved to ₹880 crore, and ICRA upgraded rating to BBB+ (Stable)

• 🤝 Signed a 5-year manufacturing services agreement with JSW Energy to ensure business continuity

🟢 Impact: Positive (Unlocks shareholder value, removes a loss-making asset, sharpens focus on the core business, and provides direct exposure to JSW Energy.)

HAVELLS INDIA: APPROVES ₹255 CRORE CABLE CAPACITY EXPANSION AT TUMAKURU ⚡🏭

• 🏗️ Approved further expansion of cable manufacturing capacity at the Tumakuru, Karnataka facility

• 📈 Capacity to increase from 4,59,600 Kms/year to 7,34,640 Kms/year (~60% expansion)

• 💰 Additional investment of ~₹255 crore, including pending capex from the previous phase

• ✅ Existing expansion phase has already achieved 4,59,600 Kms/year capacity with ₹285 crore invested so far

• 🗓️ Expansion is targeted for completion by December 2027 and will be funded through internal accruals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Capacity expansion strengthens Havells' cable business and supports long-term growth.)

INFLAME APPLIANCES: Q1 FY27 REVENUE JUMPS 34%, CHIMNEY VOLUMES UP 42% 🔥📈

• 💰 Q1 FY27 Revenue rose 34% YoY to ₹35.6 crore

• 🌬️ Chimney volumes increased 42% YoY to 64,103 units, driven by strong customer demand

• 🏭 Capacity utilisation improved to 42.7% from 30.2% in Q1 FY26, reflecting better operational efficiency

• 🤝 Acquired 34% stake in Tricoree Machmatrix Pvt. Ltd. to strengthen capabilities in electronics, embedded systems, IoT and smart control systems

• 📊 Company revised product prices from July 2026 to offset higher raw material costs

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong volume-led growth, improved capacity utilisation and technology expansion support future earnings growth.)

SHIVALIK BIMETAL: GETS CONSENT TO OPERATE FOR PUNE FACILITY

BHEL 

Why is it actually VERY Bullish? 

By reading the raw filing directly, the numbers are nothing short of phenomenal and crush the consensus estimates:

Top-Line Blowout: BHEL posted Revenue from Operations of ₹7,697 Cr, easily beating the ₹6,801 Cr expectation. This represents a massive ~40% YoY growth, shattering the anticipated 20% growth.

Monumental Profitability: Against an estimated Net Profit of just ~₹58 Cr, BHEL delivered a staggering ₹381 Cr in Standalone PAT (~6.5x the consensus).

A Historic Turnaround: Most importantly, the company reported a massive turnaround from a ₹454 Cr loss in the same quarter last year. This quarter officially breaks an eight-year streak of Q1 losses, confirming the highly anticipated turnaround thesis driven by strong order book execution.

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS: PUNE PLANT TO SCALE EV & BATTERY COMPONENT PRODUCTION ⚡🏭

• ✅ Shivalik Bimetal Controls has announced that the Consent to Operate (CTO) received for its Pune Phase-I facility is valid till June 30, 2032, enabling phased commercial operations 

• 🚗 The Pune facility will manufacture value-added components and assemblies for automotive electrification and battery applications, including bus-bar connectors and PCBA assemblies, catering to OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers 

• 📈 The company plans to scale commercial production in line with customer qualifications, programme schedules and commercial requirements, strengthening its presence in high-precision EV components

• 🌍 Shivalik currently serves 300+ global customers through manufacturing facilities in Solan and Pune, with the new plant expected to improve wallet share and long-term revenue visibility 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The CTO-backed operational rollout strengthens Shivalik's EV and battery component manufacturing capabilities and supports long-term growth in value-added products.)

ITC HOTELS: TO ACQUIRE WELCOMHOTEL AHMEDABAD OWNER FOR ₹155 CR 🏨📈

• 💰 The acquisition values GHK at an enterprise value of ₹155 crore on a cash-free, debt-free basis, subject to customary adjustments. The investment will be made through a combination of primary subscription and secondary share purchase

• 🔒 Medi Assist has integrated UIDAI's Aadhaar Offline Verification Framework into its health benefits platforms after becoming a registered Offline Verification Seeking Entity (OVSE) 

• 🏥 The consent-based digital verification enables members to access health benefits with reduced paperwork, while lowering the risk of identity misuse, impersonation and document-related fraud 

• ⚡ The solution strengthens identity verification for insurers, employers and hospitals, helping reduce manual documentation, speed up claims processing and improve trust across the healthcare ecosystem 

• 📱 Aadhaar Offline Verification is being rolled out for cashless hospital admissions, while digital reimbursement claim verification will soon be available through the MAven app 

• 🌐 The capability will also be made available to TPAs, insurers and pension funds using Medi Assist's technology platforms, expanding secure digital identity verification across the health insurance ecosystem 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The integration enhances Medi Assist's technology platform, improves fraud prevention and strengthens its digital healthcare ecosystem, supporting long-term growth and operational efficiency.)

SIYARAM RECYCLING: BAGS ₹4.14 CR INTERNATIONAL BRASS BILLETS ORDER 🌍♻️

• 🏆 Siyaram Recycling Industries has secured an international order worth ₹4.14 crore from Green Metals FZCO for the supply of Brass Billets 

• 💰 The order is valued at ₹4,13,70,317.23 and will be executed on a fixed-cost basis 

• 📅 The company expects to complete execution within 7 days, supporting near-term revenue recognition 

• 🌍 The contract has been awarded by an international customer, strengthening the company's export order book 

• ✅ The company confirmed that the order is not a related-party transaction, and the promoter/promoter group has no interest in the customer entity 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The international order strengthens Siyaram Recycling's export business, enhances revenue visibility and supports near-term growth.)

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS: RECEIVES MPCB CONSENT TO OPERATE FOR PUNE PHASE-I PLANT 🏭✅

• ✅ Shivalik Bimetal Controls has received Consent to Operate (CTO) from the Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB) for its Pune Facility (Phase-I) 

• 🏭 The approval has been granted under the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 and the Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981, along with authorization under the Hazardous and Other Wastes Management Rules, 2016 

• 📅 The approval was issued by MPCB through its letter dated July 15, 2026, following the company's earlier disclosure on May 18, 2026 

• 🚀 The regulatory clearance enables the company to commence operations at the Phase-I Pune facility, supporting capacity expansion and future production growth 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Consent to Operate is a key regulatory milestone that enables commercial operations at the new Pune facility, supporting Shivalik's manufacturing expansion.)

AURUM PROPTECH: TO ACQUIRE HOUSING.COM IN ₹458 CR SHARE-SWAP DEAL 🏠🤝

• 🏡 Aurum PropTech's Board has approved the acquisition of 100% stake in Locon Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Housing.com) from REA India Pte. Ltd. through a share-swap transaction valued at ₹458.06 crore, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals 

• 📈 As consideration, Aurum will issue 1.98 crore equity shares to REA on a preferential basis at ₹231.42 per share. Following the allotment, REA's stake in Aurum PropTech will increase from 5.54% to 24.90% 

• 🤖 The acquisition aims to integrate Housing.com's real estate marketplace with Aurum's PropTech ecosystem to build a unified AI- and data-driven platform connecting consumer demand, developer inventory, brokerage, rentals and property transactions 

• 📊 Housing.com owner Locon Solutions reported FY25 audited turnover of ₹687.46 crore and provides property listings, digital marketing, lead generation, home loan brokerage and advertising solutions for the real estate sector 

• 📝 The Board also approved the issue of 51 lakh convertible warrants to promoter Aurum RealEstate Developers Ltd. at ₹231.42 per warrant, amendments to the Articles of Association for tag-along rights, and scheduled an EGM on August 14, 2026 to seek shareholder approval 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition of Housing.com is a transformative move that significantly expands Aurum PropTech's digital real estate ecosystem, strengthens its AI capabilities and enhances long-term growth prospects.)

ECO HOTELS: EXPANDS FOOD & BEVERAGE PORTFOLIO WITH NEW RESTAURANT BRANDS 🍽️🏨

• 🍴 Eco Hotels & Resorts is expanding its food & beverage business with a portfolio of restaurant brands including Sahar, GG's Rooftop Lounge, Kick In The Brick and EcoSip Café, with a premium non-vegetarian restaurant to be launched soon 

• 🌱 Sahar focuses on 100% vegetarian, Satvik, No Onion-No Garlic and Jain cuisine, while GG's Rooftop Lounge offers vegetarian Pan-Asian and Continental dining with rooftop views 

• 🎶 Kick In The Brick combines global vegetarian cuisine with live entertainment and sports screenings, while EcoSip Café caters to coffee lovers with artisan beverages and café offerings 

• 🚀 The upcoming premium non-vegetarian restaurant will feature Indian regional cuisines and international favourites, marking the next phase of the company's F&B expansion strategy 

• 🤝 The company said the expanded restaurant portfolio is aimed at attracting both hotel guests and local diners, strengthening its hospitality ecosystem and enhancing customer experience 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The expansion of proprietary restaurant brands diversifies revenue streams, enhances the guest experience and strengthens Eco Hotels' hospitality offerings.)

MOBAVENUE AI TECH: OPENS SINGAPORE HUB TO EXPAND AI-POWERED ADTECH ACROSS ASEAN 🌏🤖

• 🇸🇬 Mobavenue AI Tech has commenced operations in Singapore, establishing the city-state as its regional hub for Southeast Asia as part of its international expansion strategy 

• 🚀 Operations will be led through MAITL Asia Pte. Ltd., enabling the company to scale its AI-powered programmatic media, audience intelligence, omnichannel customer engagement and consumer growth solutions across ASEAN 

• 📈 The company will adopt an asset-light, platform-led model, combining its proprietary AI technology with local market expertise to deliver scalable, outcome-driven advertising solutions 

• 🌍 The Singapore hub strengthens Mobavenue's ability to serve clients across ASEAN and supports its ambition of building a globally integrated AI-powered AdTech ecosystem 

• 🤝 Mobavenue currently serves 150+ brands across sectors including e-commerce, BFSI, fintech, travel, OTT, gaming, healthcare and retail, with operations spanning ASEAN, LATAM, the UK and the USA 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Singapore expansion strengthens Mobavenue's international footprint, enhances access to the fast-growing ASEAN digital advertising market and supports long-term growth.)

SHRIRAM AMC: LAUNCHES REFRESHED FUND OPPORTUNITY FOR 3 HYBRID FUNDS 📈💼

• 🔄 Shriram Asset Management Company has announced a Refreshed Fund Opportunity (RFO) for its three hybrid funds, introducing a refreshed investment strategy and framework. The Partner Opportunity Offer Period is open from July 15 to August 7, 2026 for mutual fund distributors 

• 📊 The refreshed offering covers the Shriram Multi Asset Allocation Fund, Shriram Aggressive Hybrid Fund, and Shriram Balanced Advantage Fund, with a focus on diversified exposure across equity, debt, gold and silver to help manage portfolio risk across market cycles 

• 🧠 The new framework incorporates structured asset allocation, GARP (Growth at a Reasonable Price)-based equity selection, research-led fixed income, and gold & silver exposure, creating a disciplined and repeatable investment process 

• 🎯 The refreshed hybrid funds are designed to support long-term financial goals such as retirement, children's education, home ownership and wealth creation through dynamic asset allocation 

• 💬 Management said the refresh reflects its belief that asset allocation, rather than market timing, is the key driver of long-term investor outcomes, while simplifying investment decisions for investors 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The refreshed fund strategy strengthens Shriram AMC's product offerings and could support improved investor participation and long-term asset growth.)

• 🏡 Prestige Estates reported Q1 FY27 residential pre-sales of ₹6,579 crore, with 6.04 million sq. ft. sold across 3,337 units 

• 🌆 Hyderabad contributed 49% of quarterly sales, followed by Bengaluru (27%), Mumbai (12%), NCR (7%) and other markets (5%), reflecting a diversified geographic mix 

• 💰 Customer collections remained strong at ₹4,802 crore, while the company launched four projects with a combined 20.16 million sq. ft. of developable area, including Prestige Golden Grove in Hyderabad

TCS: LAUNCHES GOOGLE CLOUD GEMINI EXPERIENCE CENTER IN KOLKATA 🤖☁️

• 🤝 TCS and Google Cloud have launched a Consumer Business Group (CBG) Gemini Experience Center in Kolkata to accelerate adoption of agentic AI across consumer businesses 

• 🌍 The Kolkata facility is TCS' eighth Gemini Experience Center globally and third in India. The company plans to establish 10 Gemini Experience Centers worldwide by the end of 2026, including four in India 

• 🛍️ The center will help enterprises co-create AI solutions for retail, consumer packaged goods (CPG), travel, tourism and hospitality, covering customer engagement, operations, pricing and commerce 

• 🧠 TCS has already built 3,000 industry- and context-aware AI agents using Gemini Enterprise, with specialized agents for retail, CPG, travel, transportation and hospitality 

• ⚙️ The center will showcase AI-powered solutions across store operations, supply chain management, omnichannel retail, customer service, HR and talent acquisition, helping clients move from AI pilots to enterprise-scale deployment 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The launch strengthens TCS' AI capabilities and deepens its strategic partnership with Google Cloud, supporting enterprise AI adoption and long-term digital transformation opportunities.)

MOBAVENUE AI TECH: OPENS SINGAPORE HUB TO EXPAND AI-POWERED ADTECH ACROSS ASEAN 🌏🤖

• 🇸🇬 Mobavenue AI Tech has commenced operations in Singapore, establishing the city-state as its regional hub for Southeast Asia as part of its international expansion strategy 

• 🚀 Operations will be led through MAITL Asia Pte. Ltd., enabling the company to scale its AI-powered programmatic media, audience intelligence, omnichannel customer engagement and consumer growth solutions across ASEAN 

• 📈 The company will adopt an asset-light, platform-led model, combining its proprietary AI technology with local market expertise to deliver scalable, outcome-driven advertising solutions 

• 🌍 Mobavenue said the Singapore hub strengthens its ability to serve clients across ASEAN and supports its vision of building a globally integrated AI-powered AdTech ecosystem 

• 🤝 The company currently serves 150+ brands across sectors including e-commerce, BFSI, fintech, travel, OTT, gaming, healthcare and retail, with operations spanning ASEAN, LATAM, the UK and the USA 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The Singapore expansion strengthens Mobavenue's international presence, enhances access to the fast-growing ASEAN digital advertising market and supports long-term growth.)

ACME SOLAR: RAISES ₹2,646.64 CR PROJECT FUNDING FROM REC FOR 450 MW FDRE PROJECT ☀️💰

• 💵 ACME Solar has secured ₹2,646.64 crore in long-term project funding from REC Ltd for its 450 MW / 1,800 MWh Firm & Dispatchable Renewable Energy (FDRE) Project 

• 🏦 REC Ltd will act as the sole lender for the project with a 20-year financing tenure 

• 📜 The project has a 25-year Power Purchase Agreement (PPA) with SJVN Ltd at a tariff of ₹6.74 per unit 

• 🔋 The FDRE project combines solar power and Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) technologies to deliver reliable and dispatchable renewable energy 

• ⚡ ACME Solar currently has a diversified portfolio of 8,070 MW, including 2,990 MW operational capacity, 5,080 MW under construction, and ~18 GWh of BESS under development 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The large project financing strengthens execution visibility, supports capacity expansion and reinforces ACME Solar's growth in the FDRE segment.)

For nearly 2 years, one of the biggest drags on India's economy wasn't weak consumption or lower government spending.
It was industrial credit growth.
Businesses simply weren't borrowing enough to build new factories, buy machinery or expand capacity & this now appears to be changing.
The latest data shows industrial credit growth has started picking up again after a prolonged slowdown. It may not make the front page every day, but for long-term investors, this is one of the more encouraging signs in the economy today.

Why does this matter?

When businesses borrow money, it is usually to invest in productive assets. They build factories, purchase machines, expand production lines and increase working capital to execute larger orders.
In simple words, today's credit often becomes tomorrow's production. This creates a positive chain reaction across the economy.

Higher industrial borrowing leads to more capital expenditure & it creates demand for machinery, transformers, cables, bearings, automation equipment, construction machinery, logistics and engineering services.

As businesses expand, more hiring gets done. Higher employment supports higher incomes, which eventually leads to stronger consumption. That is how an investment cycle gradually gathers momentum.

The timing is encouraging because India is already benefiting from several long-term trends.

• Manufacturing is gaining momentum under the China+1 opportunity.
• Government infrastructure spending remains strong.
• Power transmission and distribution investments continue to rise.
• Electronics manufacturing is expanding rapidly.
• Private sector capital expenditure is slowly making a comeback.

A recovery in industrial credit can provide another layer of support to all these themes but we all know that rising credit growth alone is not enough.

We also need to understand that the quality of lending matters just as much. If businesses are borrowing to create productive assets that generate future cash flows, the long-term impact on the economy is positive. Reckless lending, on the other hand, eventually creates bad loans.

Fortunately, India's banking system is far healthier today than it was a decade ago. Balance sheets are stronger, NPAs have fallen sharply and most quality banks have maintained disciplined lending standards.
For investors, industrial credit is worth watching because it reflects business confidence. Companies don't borrow billions of rupees unless they believe demand will remain healthy over the next few years.

If this trend continues, it could support earnings growth across sectors such as capital goods, industrials, electrical equipment, power infrastructure, engineering and quality financials.

Sometimes, the most important changes in the economy happen very very gradually & a sustained recovery in industrial credit could be one of them.

• 📑 Executed related Securities Purchase and Transaction Agreements with investors to facilitate the resolution process

• 💰 Resolution plan involves payment of ₹381.84 crore towards the resolution amount and ₹23.59 crore towards additional interest (subject to Supreme Court orders)

• ⚖️ Implementation remains subject to Supreme Court directions and fulfilment of agreed conditions precedent

• 🚫 Agreements are not related-party transactions and do not grant special rights over the company's capital structure

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marks a key step toward resolving the long-pending SHPL matter, though final implementation remains subject to Supreme Court approval.)

HCL INFOSYSTEMS: SUPREME COURT DISMISSES ₹14.90 CRORE TAX CASE ⚖️

• ✅ Supreme Court dismissed the Income Tax Department's SLP, upholding HCL Infosystems' position

• 💰 Dispute related to ₹14.90 crore tax demand on a ₹60.80 crore compensation received from HP under a terminated JV agreement

• 📌 Company had treated the compensation as a capital receipt not liable to tax

• 🏛️ ITAT and Delhi High Court had earlier ruled in HCL Infosystems' favour, now affirmed by the Supreme Court

🟢 Impact: Positive (Final legal victory removes tax uncertainty and eliminates the ₹14.90 crore tax demand.)

KNACK PACKAGING: APPROVES LEASE TO EXPAND MANUFACTURING CAPACITY 🏭

• 🏗️ Board approved sub-lease of factory land & building and lease of plant & machinery from Dayana Polyplast Ltd

• 📈 Proposed capacity addition of 5,040 MT per annum, taking place immediately after execution of agreements and required approvals

• ⚙️ Existing installed capacity stands at 43,300 MT per annum with 81.63% utilisation

• 💰 Expansion to be funded through internal accruals and/or working capital; no major capex required for land, building or machinery

• 🚀 Expansion aims to meet rising demand, improve operational efficiency, optimise production timelines and strengthen market presence

🟢 Impact: Positive (Capacity expansion with minimal capital investment supports future growth and profitability.)

GMR AIRPORTS: Q1 FY27 PASSENGER TRAFFIC CROSSES 30 MILLION ✈️

• 👥 Served 30.22 million passengers in Q1 FY27, up 0.2% YoY; June 2026 traffic stood at 9.76 million

• 🌍 International traffic grew 10%+, while domestic traffic remained broadly stable

• 🛫 Achieved a record Q1 passenger traffic across the GMR airport network

• 📦 Delhi Airport reported its highest-ever Q1 domestic passengers, cargo volumes and aircraft movements

• 🌐 Hyderabad Airport recorded its highest international passenger traffic and aircraft movements since February 2026

🟢 Impact: Positive (Record passenger traffic and resilient international growth support airport revenue and long-term growth.)

HERO MOTOCORP: ATHER APPROVES ₹960 CRORE PREFERENTIAL WARRANT ISSUE ⚡

• 💰 Ather approved allotment of 7.62 crore convertible warrants to Hero MotoCorp on a preferential basis

• 📌 Issue price fixed at ₹1,260 per warrant, aggregating to ~₹960 crore

• 🔄 Each warrant is convertible into 1 equity share within 18 months

• 📈 Hero MotoCorp's stake in Ather is expected to increase from 29.48% to 30.68% (fully diluted basis), assuming full conversion

• 💳 Hero will pay 25% upfront at subscription and the remaining 75% on warrant conversion

🟢 Impact: Positive (Reinforces Hero MotoCorp's strategic investment in Ather and strengthens its position in the fast-growing EV segment.)

ARISINFRA: BAGS ₹79.05 CRORE GMLR PROJECT ORDER 🏗️

• 📑 Secured a ₹79.05 crore work order from J. Kumar – NCC (GMLR) JV

• 🚚 Scope includes loading, transportation and disposal of excavated tunnel material at the approved dumping yard for the GMLR Project

• 🇮🇳 Order awarded by a domestic entity

• ⏳ Contract remains valid until completion of excavated material disposal from the GMLR tunnelling activity or as per contract terms

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Arisinfra's order book and provides revenue visibility from a major infrastructure project.)

Unihealth Hospitals continues to execute on its East Africa strategy - Commissions its Eye Care & Ophthalmology Department at UMC Victoria Hospital, Uganda

UMC Victoria Hospital, Kampala has commissioned its dedicated Eye Care & Ophthalmology Department, with the first cataract surgeries completed successfully. This follows shortly after the Board approved increasing UniHealth's stake in Victoria Hospital Limited from 50.00% to 99.81% through a strategic share-swap transaction.

✔️ Adds a new high-value specialty with significant long-term growth potential.

✔️ Management plans to expand into Phaco Surgery, Retinal Surgery, Glaucoma Management, Paediatric Ophthalmology and advanced diagnostics, positioning the hospital as a regional eye-care centre.

✔️ As UniHealth moves towards near-complete ownership of Victoria Hospital, future earnings from such specialty expansions are expected to accrue substantially to UniHealth shareholders.

✔️ The development reflects management's strategy of pairing ownership consolidation with continuous investment in higher-margin clinical services.

East Africa already contributes the majority of UniHealth's consolidated revenues, making developments at Victoria Hospital particularly relevant from a long-term shareholder perspective.

• 🏭 Riddhi Display Equipments has commenced commercial operations at its new manufacturing facility in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh

CSM TECHNOLOGIES: WINS WORLD BANK-FUNDED DIGITAL PROCUREMENT PROJECT IN MALAWI 🌍💻

• 🌍 CSM Technologies has secured a World Bank-funded international assignment from the Public Private Partnership Commission (PPPC) of Malawi to develop an Electronic Auctioning Platform (EAP) under the Digital Malawi Acceleration Project (DMAP)

• 💰 The project, funded by the World Bank's International Development Association (IDA), is valued at ₹3.21 crore (USD 0.32 million / ₹32.08 million)

• ⚙️ CSM will design, develop and commission a secure digital procurement platform featuring online bid submission, real-time evaluation, bidder anonymity and automated publication of results

• 🏛️ The platform aims to improve transparency and efficiency in public procurement, benefiting over 75 lakh broadband users, 2,000 schools and 500 government offices in Malawi

• 🤝 The company will execute the entire project lifecycle, including system development, integration, cybersecurity, testing, rollout, user training and post-deployment support

🟢 Impact: Positive (The World Bank-backed international contract strengthens CSM Technologies' global GovTech presence, enhances execution credentials and supports future overseas business opportunities.)

Recode Studios Acquires 51% Stake in Premium Beauty Brand Aflairza

- Aflairza operates in the premium beauty segment with strong consumer acceptance

- The brand has demonstrated superior value realization, reflecting its premium positioning

- Acquisition aligns with Recode's strategy to expand its presence in the high-growth Beauty & Personal Care category

- Company will also utilize its distribution network and operational expertise to scale the brand

- Founders will continue to partner with Recode in the next phase of expansion

It gives Recode a stronger presence in the premium beauty segment, where higher pricing power and better margins can create long-term value. Leveraging Recode's distribution, product development and execution capabilities could significantly accelerate Aflairza's scale over the coming years.

Recode will initially acquire a 33% stake in Phase I for an aggregate consideration of ₹8.32 Crore, with equal allocation between promoter share purchase & fresh capital into the company.

Recode Studios Acquires 51% Stake in Premium Beauty Brand Aflairza

- Aflairza operates in the premium beauty segment with strong consumer acceptance

- The brand has demonstrated superior value realization, reflecting its premium positioning

- Acquisition aligns with Recode's strategy to expand its presence in the high-growth Beauty & Personal Care category

- Company will also utilize its distribution network and operational expertise to scale the brand

- Founders will continue to partner with Recode in the next phase of expansion

It gives Recode a stronger presence in the premium beauty segment, where higher pricing power and better margins can create long-term value. Leveraging Recode's distribution, product development and execution capabilities could significantly accelerate Aflairza's scale over the coming years.

Recode will initially acquire a 33% stake in Phase I for an aggregate consideration of ₹8.32 Crore, with equal allocation between promoter share purchase & fresh capital into the company.

🇮🇳 Is India losing the global innovation race?

India's innovation lag isn't a govt failure; it's a corporate issue. The country spends just 0.65% of GDP on R&D, vastly below the 2.5-3% global norm for major economies.

Over 60% of R&D is state-funded. Meanwhile, top IT firms returned over ₹6tn to shareholders in the last decade instead of funding tech, choosing short-term margins over long-term R&D.

Tactical play: Pivot away from stagnating giants. Bet on deep-tech startups & niche mid-caps. The entry of foreign players & new competition will act as the catalyst for growth.

 🎯 Bottom Line: Avoid low-growth incumbents returning cash over innovating. Markets will heavily reward companies prioritizing long-term R&D with superior valuation multiples.

CITI on OMCs

Citi’s global commodities team highlights that recent disruptions across Middle East, China, and Russia have tightened global product balances despite relatively resilient crude supply, supporting elevated refining cracks

This dynamic of soft crude prices but high refining cracks augurs well for OMCs’ integrated margins (IOCL>BPCL>HPCL), assuming status quo on excise duties, export taxes, and retail fuel prices.

Separately, ONGC too has been called upon for national service following directions from the gov’t to develop an SPR facility
Prefer downstream OMCs over upstream SOEs.

Buy, TP Rs 3985

Co’s strength in performance across major large schemes, diversified product bouquet (gross non-MF revenue mix at ~17%) & robust product pipeline in non-MF positions it in a sweet spot to leverage on incremental retail/HNI penetration in the nascent MF ecosystem.

#HEG | Demerger Special Situation

Sometimes the market does not value a company properly because too many different businesses sit inside one structure.

Stay till the end and let us know which of the demerged business you would want to be in!!

-------------------

HEG looks like one such case.

Today, HEG is a mix of:

• Graphite electrodes  
• Hydro power  
• Battery anode materials  
• Battery energy storage solutions  
• Solar + BESS renewable platform  

Now the company is splitting this into two focused listed entities.

1. HEG Limited

This will house the mature graphite electrode business.

FY26 revenue: ~₹2,568 Cr  
FY26 EBITDA: ~₹497 Cr  
EBITDA margin: ~19%

Capacity is being expanded from 1,00,000 TPA to 1,15,000 TPA by early 2028.

This remains the cash-generating industrial business.

2. HEG Greentech

This will house the future-facing clean energy platform.

It includes:

• 278 MW hydro assets  
• TACC battery anode materials  
• REPlus battery energy solutions  
• Solar + BESS IPP platform  

The interesting part?

The hydro assets alone are expected to generate ~₹300 Cr+ annual free cash flow.

So Greentech is not starting from zero.

3. The big optionality

TACC is setting up India’s first large-scale battery-grade synthetic graphite anode material facility.

Phase 1: 20,000 TPA  
Commercial start: April 2027  
Long-term target: 60,000 TPA by FY32

If this executes well, the market may stop looking at HEG only as a graphite electrode company.

4. Why this can create opportunity

Right now, the market is valuing one combined entity.

But post-demerger, investors may get two separate stories:

• A mature graphite electrode business  
• A hydro-backed Greentech platform  

That separation itself can unlock better valuation discovery.

5. Near-term trigger

The key event to watch is NCLT approval.

Management’s latest guidance is Q2 FY27.

Post that, the focus shifts to record date, listing timeline and separate valuation of both entities.

Simple takeaway:

HEG demerger is not just a split.

It is a chance for the market to revalue two very different businesses separately.

One is cash-generating.

The other is optionality-heavy.

That is what makes this special situation worth tracking.

Not a recommendation.

CITI On Grasim
Grasim’s 71% subsidiary Aditya Birla Renewables (ABRL) will acquire 100% in Solene Power at an EV of Rs172bn, to be funded via debt and equity.
SPPL has 5GWp of renewable power.
Implications for Grasim: Assume D/E ratio of 80:20, implying equity of Rs34bn.
Equity infusion would be from Grasim and Global Infrastructure Partners (Blackrock)
Are unclear on Grasim’s equity contribution
Think Grasim would like to balance capital allocation with scaling up business
They will likely infuse below 71% of Rs34bn & dilute their stake in ABRL (retain majority).
Standalone net D/EBITDA was 3.9x in FY26; net debt at Rs68.8bn.
Dividend inflow from ULTC of Rs40bn does not help – largely used for Rs28.8bn equity invested in AB Capital and Rs6.8bn payout by Grasim
Understand Grasim will raise debt; pending clarity on impact on Grasim’s leverage, stock could react negatively.

Core PBT (adjusted for one-off employee expense reversals in 4QFY26 and post incorporating impact of merger of select schemes of ICICI Ventures) was largely flat QoQ.
MF AAUM was +1% QoQ reflecting drag in equity AAUM due to softness in equity MTM.
Growth in PMS/AIF AAUM (adjusted) was strong at ~8.1%/6.5% QoQ owing to strength in flows and differentiated MTM across various strategies in PMS/equity MF.
Calculated MF yields were down 0.22 bps QoQ even as the company indicated lack of any impact post change in TER from Apr‘26
Co’s strength in performance across major large schemes, diversified product bouquet (gross non-MF revenue mix at ~17%) & robust product pipeline in non-MF positions it in a sweet spot to leverage on incremental retail/HNI penetration in the nascent MF ecosystem.

CLSA on ICICI Pru AMC
O-P, TP Rs 3650
1QFY27 PAT of Rs9.6bn, 5% higher than 3QFY26 levels
While revenue grew a modest 1% QoQ, operating profit declined 3% sequentially due to higher employee costs (4QFY26 had a low base).
In operating environment, it was largely business as usual.
Equity flows for the industry were only slightly lower at Rs1.1tn, while industry SIP flows were stable.
For IPRU AMC, monthly SIP flows moderated 5% QoQ to Rs49bn but were still 15% higher than YoY levels.
Equity quarterly-average AUM (QAAUM) increased 2% QoQ, similar to prior quarter, to Rs6.3bn
Trim FY27-29 PAT estimates by 3-4% on higher opex

HSBC on ICICI Pru AMC
Buy, TP Rs 3800
1QFY27 PAT beat on back of higher-than-expected treasury gains; core performance was largely in line with HSBCe
While there was AUM market share loss, a large part of it was driven by differential in MTM and muted SIP trends
See re-rating potential on back of 22% EPS CAGR (FY26- 29e) and 1.4x PEG

UBS on Waaree Energies
Downgrade to Neutral from Buy, TP cut to Rs 3100 from Rs 4400
Lower target multiple of 21x 12M fwd PE (from 28x)
Downgrade incorporates a higher discount to industry/sector peer average & lower margins, driven by near-/medium-term challenges amid ongoing sector consolidation
Waaree’s Rs300bn capex plan (vs Rs130bn last year) is expected to scale its capacity to 15.4GW modules & 10GW cell/ingot–wafer by FY27/28E (20% of total domestic capacity).
While this strategy should help Waaree sustain its sector leadership with higher level of integration, think risk to sectoral profitability from significant new capacity, as
well as execution of its ambitious capex plan & limited clarity on potential returns in nascent segments such as BESS, are variables that need further clarity

**SESHAASAI TECHNOLOGIES | Investec View**

**Brokerage View**
- Accumulate rating.
- Target Price: ₹430.
- Quality annuity franchise.
- Emerging growth engine.

**Key Highlights**
- Oligopolistic payment card market.
- High entry barriers.
- Payment cards remain relevant.
- IoT is new growth engine.
- Steady margin outlook.
- Healthy cash conversion.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Investec remains positive on Seshaasai Technologies, citing its resilient payment card franchise, emerging IoT opportunity and strong cash generation to support long-term growth.**

**OIL & GAS SECTOR | Citi View**

**Sector View**
- Prefer downstream over upstream.
- Soft crude outlook.
- Strong refining environment.

**Key Highlights**
- Global product balances tightening.
- Crude supply remains resilient.
- ONGC to develop SPR facility.
- National service assignment.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi prefers downstream oil marketing companies over upstream producers, supported by a favorable refining outlook despite softer crude prices.**

Aerospace Contract Manufacturing is structurally superior to Pharma CDMO.

Both are Contract Manufacturing businesses at their core, but the quality and duration of earnings are very different.

A Pharma CDMO molecule typically offers 10-15 years of visibility before patent expiry creates revenue reset risk.

An aerospace programme can run for 50-70 years, offers 4-5x longer revenue visibility, carries prohibitive switching costs and generates decades of aftermarket revenues.

Longer visibility. Lower reset risk. Stickier customers. More durable earnings.

That is why aerospace contract manufacturing deserves higher value.

Source: Nuvama Research, Aequs initiation report.

**PRIVI SPECIALITY CHEMICALS | InCred View**

**Key Takeaway**

**Key Highlights**
- Encouraging asset-quality trends.
- 2W segment improving.
- AI engines supporting underwriting.
- Risks: Monsoon.
- Risks: Insurance distribution reforms.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains positive on L&T Finance, supported by better-than-expected Q1FY27 earnings, improving asset quality and higher earnings estimates despite near-term risks.**

**CARTRADE | UBS Initiates Coverage**

**Brokerage View**
- UBS initiates with Buy.
- Target Price: ₹4,000.
- Asset-light business model.
- Significant operating leverage.
- 15–20% earnings upside.
- FY27–FY29E consensus estimates.

**Growth & Margin Outlook**
- Revenue at only 3–4% of TAM.
- Significant top-line growth potential.
- OLX dominates used C2C platform.
- FY23 margin: 9%.
- FY26 margin: 33%.
- FY30E margin: 47%.
- Global peers: 50–60% margins.

**Key Takeaway**
- **UBS expects CarTrade's asset-light model, margin expansion, and large untapped market opportunity to drive strong earnings growth over the coming years.**

Goldman Sachs: India Strategy | Room To Rebound

India strategy turned more positive: Target Nifty 26,500 by Jun'27 with expectation of FII flows returning and domestic recovery strengthening #ym

*Key calls are* Value over Growth, Large Caps over Mid Caps, Banks/Utilities/Tourism/Defense as preferred themes, while Metals, Cement, IT/Pharma exporters and rural plays are less favored 

Sector changes: 

*Upgraded
> Utilities → Overweight 
> Industrials → Market Weight 

*Downgraded
> Staples → Market Weight (from a more positive stance) 
> Metals & Mining → Underweight 
> Cement → Underweight 

*Unchanged positive
> Banks → Overweight
> Energy Refiners → Overweight 
> TMT → Overweight 
> Defense → Overweight 

*Unchanged negative
> IT Exporters → Underweight 
> Pharma Exporters → Underweight
> OMCs/Downstream Oil → Underweight

Goldman Sachs -India Strategy: Room to rebound  
India’s outlook has improved in recent weeks, amid lower commodity prices, stabilized currency, resilient domestic growth, healthy earnings expectations in 2Q, and potential recovery in select domestic pockets. With ultra-light foreign positioning, we see ample room for flows to return. While renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East may fuel near-term volatility, we see room for NIFTY to recover towards our June 2027 target of 26,500, implying c.10% upside from current levels following a 9% drawdown in 1H

Goldman Sachs - India Strategy: Room to rebound
Structurally, we remain bullish on defense and energy security. Sectorally, we raise utilities to overweight and remain overweight on banks, energy refiners, TMT, and defense. We
stay underweight exporters, downstream oil and downgrade select materials to underweight
